title: Restaurant Operating System - Project Architecture & MVP Planning
summary: The meeting presented the complete architecture and phased roadmap for a centralized operational management platform targeting small restaurants. The system is built on four universal entities (Person, Document, Event, Resource) that compose all business functions without rigid modules. MVP Phase 1 covers employee management, task assignment with WhatsApp notifications, document tracking with expiry alerts, and basic inventory. A Phase 2 extension was detailed for recipe standardization, automatic cost calculation via invoice OCR scanning, and profitability alerts.

discussion_points.description: The entire system is built on 4 universal entities: Person (employees, suppliers, owners), Document (contracts, permits, invoices), Event (tasks, reminders, payments, shifts, checklists), and Resource (products, assets, services). All business functions are composed from combinations of these entities rather than rigid modules.

Small restaurants suffer from operational disorder: unclear task ownership, expired permits and documents, inventory discrepancies, uncontrolled waste, and forgotten payments/taxes. Current tools (WhatsApp, notebooks, memory, disconnected apps) are insufficient. POS systems only handle sales; ERPs are expensive and rigid.

Phase 1 delivers: employee directory, task lists with date/frequency assignment, daily WhatsApp task notifications per employee, document upload with expiration dates, automatic pre-expiry alerts, and basic inventory with entry/exit movements (purchase, usage, production, waste).

The Event entity serves as the system's nervous system. All key flows pass through Events: documents with expiry dates create Events, Events trigger notifications on their dates, recurring tasks generate daily Events, and inventory changes register movements. Notification channels include email, push, and WhatsApp.

A Phase 2 extension adds Recipe and Recipe_Item entities on top of existing Resource/Document/Event/Inventory_Movement. Recipes define theoretical dish costs by linking resources with quantities. When ingredient costs change (via invoice scanning), all affected recipe costs are automatically recalculated.

Supplier invoices are photographed and uploaded as Documents. AI/OCR extracts supplier, products, quantities, and unit prices. Each detected item updates the corresponding Resource's latest unit cost and registers an inventory entry movement. Cost changes trigger automatic recipe recalculation.

Each Recipe tracks current cost, margin percentage, and a profitability flag. When margin drops below a defined minimum (e.g., 60%), the system creates an Event of type 'profitability_alert' notifying the owner via push/WhatsApp with the dish name, cost, sale price, and margin.

Document + Event combinations handle regulatory compliance: food handling permits (30-day advance alert), fire department permits (expiry alert), commerce chamber registration (annual alert), fire extinguisher recharges (recharge alert), and health inspections (inspection alert).

Business functions are not rigid modules but entity combinations: Contracts = Person + Document + Event, Permits = Document + Event, Checklists = Event + Person, Inventory = Resource + Event, Payments = Event + Document, Payroll = Person + Event, Purchasing = Resource + Event, Marketing = Event + AI, Production = Resource + Event, Franchises = same model per restaurant.

Proposed stack: PostgreSQL via Supabase for database, REST or GraphQL API, React or FlutterFlow for frontend, Email + WhatsApp API for notifications, and DocuSign API for digital signatures in Phase 2.
Phase 1: CRUD for all 4 entities, notifications, basic inventory. Phase 2: Digital contracts, basic payroll, production/cost centers. Phase 3: AI for prediction, analysis, and autonomous agents.

action_items.description: Build CRUD operations for the 4 core entities: Person, Document, Event, Resource
Implement notification system supporting email, push, and WhatsApp channels
Build Inventory_Movement tracking (entries/exits with motives: purchase, usage, production, waste)
Implement event-driven automation: document expiry creates Events, recurring tasks generate daily Events
Design and implement Recipe and Recipe_Item data models for cost management
Build invoice scanning pipeline with OCR for automatic cost extraction and Resource updates
Implement automatic recipe cost recalculation when ingredient prices change
Build profitability alert system with configurable margin thresholds
Optionally convert project overview into architecture diagram and technical backlog

decisions: The system will be built on 4 universal entities (Person, Document, Event, Resource) rather than rigid modules
Event entity serves as the central nervous system — all workflows pass through Events
MVP Phase 1 scope: employee management, task assignment with WhatsApp notifications, document tracking with expiry alerts, and basic inventory
Recipe and cost management is explicitly deferred to Phase 2 as it depends on established inventory, defined recipes, digitized invoices, and minimum operational discipline
No sub-inventories or complex cost centers in Phase 1 — kept deliberately simple
Technology stack: PostgreSQL (Supabase), REST/GraphQL API, React/FlutterFlow frontend, WhatsApp + Email notifications
The platform is positioned as a 'daily operating system for small restaurants' — not a POS or ERP
Design principle: 'We don't design functions. We design a system that enables functions.'
next_steps: Set up PostgreSQL database on Supabase with the 4 core entity schemas plus Inventory_Movement
Build Phase 1 MVP with CRUD, notifications, and basic inventory
Convert project overview into a formal architecture diagram
Create a technical development backlog from the phased roadmap
Validate Phase 1 with real small restaurant operations before proceeding to Phase 2

diagrams.title: Core Entity Architecture
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Universal Entities
        P["Person\n(employees, suppliers, owners)"]
        D["Document\n(contracts, permits, invoices)"]
        E["Event\n(tasks, reminders, payments, shifts)"]
        R["Resource\n(products, assets, services)"]
    end

    subgraph Relationships
        P -->|has| D
        P -->|responsible for| E
        D -->|triggers| E
        R -->|tracked by| E
        R -->|referenced in| D
    end

    subgraph Inventory
        IM["Inventory_Movement\n(entry/exit with motive)"]
        R -->|stock changes| IM
        P -->|registers| IM
    end

    style P fill:#4A90D9,stroke:#2C5F8A,color:#fff
    style D fill:#7B68EE,stroke:#5A4CB5,color:#fff
    style E fill:#E8833A,stroke:#B8652D,color:#fff
    style R fill:#50C878,stroke:#3A9458,color:#fff
    style IM fill:#FFD700,stroke:#B8960A,color:#333

diagrams.title: Event-Driven System Flow
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart LR
    subgraph Triggers
        DOC["Document\nwith expiry date"]
        TASK["Recurring Task\nwith frequency"]
        INV["Inventory Change"]
        RECIPE["Margin Below\nThreshold"]
    end

    DOC -->|creates| EVT["EVENT"]
    TASK -->|generates daily| EVT
    INV -->|registers| MOV["Inventory\nMovement"]
    RECIPE -->|creates| EVT

    EVT -->|date arrives| NOTIFY{"Notification\nEngine"}

    NOTIFY -->|channel| EMAIL["Email"]
    NOTIFY -->|channel| PUSH["Push"]
    NOTIFY -->|channel| WA["WhatsApp"]

    style EVT fill:#E8833A,stroke:#B8652D,color:#fff
    style NOTIFY fill:#FF6B6B,stroke:#CC5555,color:#fff
    style EMAIL fill:#4A90D9,stroke:#2C5F8A,color:#fff
    style PUSH fill:#4A90D9,stroke:#2C5F8A,color:#fff
    style WA fill:#25D366,stroke:#1DA851,color:#fff

diagrams.title: Module Composition from Entities
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    P["Person"] 
    D["Document"]
    E["Event"]
    R["Resource"]
    AI["AI Engine"]

    P & D & E --> CONTRACTS["Contracts"]
    D & E --> PERMITS["Permits"]
    E & P --> CHECKLISTS["Checklists"]
    R & E --> INVENTORY["Inventory"]
    E & D --> PAYMENTS["Payments"]
    P & E --> PAYROLL["Payroll"]
    R & E --> PURCHASING["Purchasing"]
    E & AI --> MARKETING["Marketing"]
    R & E --> PRODUCTION["Production"]

    style P fill:#4A90D9,stroke:#2C5F8A,color:#fff
    style D fill:#7B68EE,stroke:#5A4CB5,color:#fff
    style E fill:#E8833A,stroke:#B8652D,color:#fff
    style R fill:#50C878,stroke:#3A9458,color:#fff
    style AI fill:#FF69B4,stroke:#CC5490,color:#fff
    style CONTRACTS fill:#F0F0F0,stroke:#999
    style PERMITS fill:#F0F0F0,stroke:#999
    style CHECKLISTS fill:#F0F0F0,stroke:#999
    style INVENTORY fill:#F0F0F0,stroke:#999
    style PAYMENTS fill:#F0F0F0,stroke:#999
    style PAYROLL fill:#F0F0F0,stroke:#999
    style PURCHASING fill:#F0F0F0,stroke:#999
    style MARKETING fill:#F0F0F0,stroke:#999
    style PRODUCTION fill:#F0F0F0,stroke:#999

diagrams.title: Invoice Scanning and Cost Update Pipeline
diagrams.mermaid_code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant SYS as System
    participant OCR as AI / OCR Engine
    participant DB as Database

    U->>SYS: Upload invoice photo
    SYS->>DB: Create Document (type: supplier_invoice)
    SYS->>OCR: Send image for extraction
    OCR-->>SYS: Return supplier, products, quantities, unit prices
    
    loop For each detected item
        SYS->>DB: Find matching Resource
        SYS->>DB: Update Resource.last_unit_cost
        SYS->>DB: Create Inventory_Movement (entry, purchase)
        SYS->>DB: Find Recipes using this Resource
        loop For each affected Recipe
            SYS->>DB: Recalculate Recipe.current_cost
            SYS->>DB: Update Recipe.margin
            alt Margin below threshold
                SYS->>DB: Create Event (profitability_alert)
                SYS->>U: Send WhatsApp/Push notification
            end
        end
    end

diagrams.title: Recipe Cost Calculation and Profitability Flow
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    REC["Recipe\n(dish name, sale price)"] -->|contains| RI["Recipe_Items\n(resource + quantity + unit)"]
    RI -->|references| RES["Resource\n(last_unit_cost)"]

    RES -->|cost data| CALC["Cost Calculation\nsum of quantity x unit_cost"]
    CALC -->|updates| COST["Recipe.current_cost"]
    
    COST --> MARGIN{"Calculate Margin\n(sale_price - cost) / sale_price"}
    
    MARGIN -->|above threshold| OK["Profitable\nNo action needed"]
    MARGIN -->|below threshold| ALERT["Create Event\ntype: profitability_alert"]
    
    ALERT --> NOTIF["Notify Owner\nvia WhatsApp / Push"]
    NOTIF --> MSG["Dish X is no longer profitable\nCost: 22,000 | Price: 30,000\nMargin: 26%"]

    style REC fill:#4A90D9,stroke:#2C5F8A,color:#fff
    style RI fill:#7B68EE,stroke:#5A4CB5,color:#fff
    style RES fill:#50C878,stroke:#3A9458,color:#fff
    style CALC fill:#FFD700,stroke:#B8960A,color:#333
    style COST fill:#FFD700,stroke:#B8960A,color:#333
    style OK fill:#50C878,stroke:#3A9458,color:#fff
    style ALERT fill:#FF6B6B,stroke:#CC5555,color:#fff
    style NOTIF fill:#E8833A,stroke:#B8652D,color:#fff

diagrams.title: Inventory Movement Lifecycle
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart LR
    subgraph Entry Sources
        PUR["Purchase\n(from supplier invoice)"]
        PROD_IN["Production\n(output)"]
    end

    subgraph Exit Sources
        USE["Usage\n(daily operations)"]
        RECIPE_OUT["Recipe Production\n(ingredient consumption)"]
        WASTE["Waste / Shrinkage\n(merma)"]
    end

    PUR -->|entry| STOCK["Resource\nstock_actual"]
    PROD_IN -->|entry| STOCK

    STOCK -->|exit| USE
    STOCK -->|exit| RECIPE_OUT
    STOCK -->|exit| WASTE

    STOCK -->|tracked via| IM["Inventory_Movement\n(resource_id, type, qty,\nmotive, date, person_id)"]

    IM -->|feeds| PREDICT["Future Prediction\nand Analytics"]
    IM -->|below minimum| ALERT_LOW["Low Stock Alert\n(Event created)"]

    style STOCK fill:#50C878,stroke:#3A9458,color:#fff
    style IM fill:#FFD700,stroke:#B8960A,color:#333
    style PREDICT fill:#7B68EE,stroke:#5A4CB5,color:#fff
    style ALERT_LOW fill:#FF6B6B,stroke:#CC5555,color:#fff
